name: Dashboard Grid
when_to_use: Use to give an at-a-glance overview of several related metrics, charts, and summaries on one screen, letting users assess overall state and then drill into specifics, such as analytics, product, and business dashboards.
when_not_to_use: Avoid when users have a single primary task (the grid scatters focus), when metrics lack relationship and become a wall of vanity numbers, or when real-time operational monitoring demands the denser command-centre pattern.
information_structure: A responsive grid of self-contained widgets (cards, charts, KPIs), each presenting one coherent piece of information, arranged by importance with the most decision-relevant items top-left.
navigation: Scan top-level tiles, then click a widget or KPI to drill into detail; global filters (date range, segment) recompute all widgets consistently.
responsive_behaviour: Reflow the grid from multi-column to single-column on narrow viewports, preserving priority order so the most important widgets appear first when stacked.
accessibility_notes: Give each widget a programmatic heading so screen-reader users can navigate between sections
Provide chart data in an accessible text or table alternative
Ensure global filter changes are announced and focus is not lost on recompute
evidence: Nielsen Norman Group dashboard guidance recommends prioritising by decision value and supporting drill-down
Google Analytics, Stripe, and product analytics tools validate the overview-plus-drill-down model
